zh.Sowerby, Hambleton district, North Yorks: 英國英格蘭越可爾瑟斯克
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard De Rode於1280年出生在英國爾諾斯 洛德時，他的父親Sir Knight Robert de Rode30歲，母親Mathilda De Watton50歲。他與Lady De Jourdain結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1350年在英國英格蘭越可爾瑟斯克享年70歲。

zh-hans.Sowerby, Hambleton district, North Yorks: 英国英格兰越可爾瑟斯克
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard De Rode于1280年出生在英国爾諾斯 洛德时，他的父亲Sir Knight Robert de Rode30岁，母亲Mathilda De Watton50岁。他与Lady De Jourdain结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1350年在英国英格兰越可爾瑟斯克享年70岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Rode
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Erwin, Ewald, Hans, Heinz, Oskar, Viktor, Claus, Frieda, Fritz, Gerhard, Horst.
German and French: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names with the first element hrōd ‘renown’. Compare Robert , Rudiger .

North German, Danish, and English: topographic name for someone who lived on land cleared for cultivation or in a clearing in woodland, from Middle Low German rode, Danish rothe, Old English rod. There are a number of places in England named with this word, for example Rode in Cheshire, and the surname may derive from any of these. Compare Rhode .

variants.name: Ruth
meanings: German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names formed with hrōd ‘renown’ (see Rode ).
(Rüth): habitational name from a place so named near Euskirchen in Rhineland.

English: from Middle English reuth(e) ‘pity’, a nickname perhaps for a charitable person or for a pitiable one. The personal name Ruth was little used in England in the Middle Ages among non-Jews, and is unlikely to have had any influence on the surname.

variants.name: Rody
meanings: German: variant of   Rode   1.
Scottish: variant of Rodie, from a pet form of Roderick .

variants.name: Robert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Gilles, Normand, Armand, Lucien, Pierre, Emile, Michel, Edouard, Gaston, Yves.

French, Walloon, English, German, Dutch, Flemish, and Breton (of French origin); Hungarian (Róbert): from the ancient Germanic personal name Ro(d)bert (Middle English Robert, Old French Ro(d)bert), composed of the elements hrōd ‘fame, renown’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This is found occasionally in Anglo-Saxon England before the Conquest, but in the main it was introduced into England by the Normans and quickly became popular among all classes of society. Pet forms and diminutives are found from an early date, Robin being particularly common. Compare Robair , Robar , Robare , and Rober .
In some cases also an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish surnames.

variants.name: Rodeman
meanings: Americanized form of German Rodemann:
topographic name for someone who lived on woodland cleared for cultivation, a variant of Rode 2 with the addition of Middle Low German man ‘man’.
from an ancient Germanic personal name, Hrodman, formed with hrōd ‘renown, fame’ + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Winterroth
meanings: German: topographic name for someone who lived on the north side of a clearing (see   Rode  ).

variants.name: Roth
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname for a person with red hair, from Middle High German rōt, German rot ‘red’. As a Jewish name it is at least in part artificial: its frequency as a Jewish surname is disproportionate to the number of Jews who, one may reasonably assume, were red-headed during the period of surname adoption. This form of the German surname (especially in this sense and in the sense 2 below) is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), where it is most common, and in some other European countries, e.g. Czechia and Croatia.

German and English (Middlesex): topographic name for someone who lived on land that had been cleared, from Old High German rod, Middle English roth(e) (Old English roth) ‘clearing’. In England, the name may also be a habitational name from any of the places like Rothend in Ashdon (Essex), Roe End in Markyate (Hertfordshire), Roe Green in Hatfield (Hertfordshire), or Roe Green in Sandon (Hertfordshire).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names with the first element hrōd ‘renown’. Compare Rode 1, Ross 4.

variants.name: Rohde
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Kurt, Otto, Bernd, Hans, Heino, Heinz, Ewald, Gerhard, Helmut, Hermann, Klaus.
German and Danish: variant of   Rode   2 and a habitational name from any of various places called Rohde or Rohden in Lower Saxony, Saxony, Westphalia, and Hesse. Compare   Rhode   and   Rhody  .

variants.name: Roady
meanings: Perhaps an Americanized form of German   Rode  .

variants.name: Rodich
meanings: Americanized form of Serbian and Croatian Rodić: patronymic from a short form of a Slavic compound personal name based on the element rod ‘family, relatives’, e.g. Rodoslav.
Americanized form of Slovenian Rodič: surname probably related to (or derived from) Rode . Alternatively, it may be of the same origin as the Serbian and Croatian name above.
German: perhaps a variant of Rohdich, from a personal name based on ancient Germanic hrōd ‘renown, victory’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard De Rode was born in 1280, in North Rode, Cheshire, England, his father, Sir Knight Robert de Rode, was 30 and his mother, Mathilda De Watton, was 50. He married Lady De Jourdain. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1350, in Thirsk, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard De Rode est né en 1280, à North Rode, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Knight Robert de Rode, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mathilda De Watton, avait 50 ans. Il a épousé Lady De Jourdain. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1350, à Thirsk, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard De Rode 1280 in North Rode, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Knight Robert de Rode 30 und seine Mutter Mathilda De Watton 50. Er hat Lady De Jourdain geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1350 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Thirsk, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Rode è nato il nel 1280 nel North Rode, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Knight Robert de Rode, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Mathilda De Watton, aveva 50 anni. Lui sposato Lady De Jourdain. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1350, nel Thirsk, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard De Rodeが1280年にイギリスチェシュアイアノース ロドで生まれた時，父Sir Knight Robert de Rodeは30歳，母Mathilda De Wattonは50歳であった。Lady De Jourdainと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1350年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアスイルスクで70歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard De Rode(이)가 1280년 영국 체실르 노르스 로드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Knight Robert de Rode(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Mathilda De Watton(은)는 50세였다. 그는 Lady De Jourdain(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1350년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 스이르스크에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard De Rode nasceu em 1280, em North Rode, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Knight Robert de Rode, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mathilda De Watton, tinha 50 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady De Jourdain. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1350, em Thirsk, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard De Rode родился в 1280 г. в местности норт род, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Knight Robert de Rode было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mathilda De Watton, 50. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady De Jourdain. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1350 г. в местности тирск, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard De Rode nació en 1280, en North Rode, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Knight Robert de Rode, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Mathilda De Watton, tenía 50 años. Se casó con Lady De Jourdain. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1350, en Thirsk, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años.
